content: We can frame Self-Refine, the task-specific iterative output refinement framework, as a concrete "workflow implementation" of the abstract cognitive foundation laid by LLM self-reflection mechanisms, and Self-Refine's ability to operate without extra training or external labels can be extended to build multi-perspective self-reflection mechanisms that also avoid costly supervised fine-tuning. This creates a fully unsupervised LLM cognitive architecture where task-specific refinement instantiates general reflective capability, rather than treating reflection as a separate trained module.
